name: Java - Spring Webservices
scope: A person with BASIC proficiency level in Java - Spring Webservices as a competency should be able to design, implement, and consume RESTful and SOAP-based web services using the Spring ecosystem within a monolithic or modular application. They write solid Java SE code applying OOP, generics, streams, lambdas, try-with-resources and use core libraries (java.util, java.time, java.io, java.nio, java.net). They understand JVM basics including class-loading, memory areas, garbage collection fundamentals and can diagnose ClassNotFoundException, OutOfMemoryError and checked-vs-unchecked exception flows. They configure Spring IoC through @Configuration classes, employ component stereotypes (@Component, @Service, @Repository, @Controller, @RestController), manage bean scopes (singleton, prototype, request, session) and lifecycle callbacks, and leverage component scanning, qualifiers, and PropertySources. They build RESTful APIs using Spring MVC with @RestController, @RequestMapping, @GetMapping, @PostMapping, @PutMapping, @DeleteMapping, binding path variables, query parameters, headers, and request bodies via @PathVariable, @RequestParam, @RequestBody. They perform JSON serialization/deserialization with Jackson (ObjectMapper, @JsonProperty, @JsonIgnore, custom serializers), handle content negotiation for JSON and XML, and apply JSR-303/380 bean validation with @Valid and BindingResult. They implement global error handling via @ControllerAdvice and @ExceptionHandler, return structured error responses with proper HTTP status codes, and understand REST principles including resource naming, HTTP methods, status codes, HATEOAS basics, and idempotency. They consume external REST APIs using RestTemplate and WebClient (basic synchronous usage), handle timeouts, retries, and error responses from downstream services, and parse external API responses into DTOs. They have introductory exposure to SOAP web services: understanding WSDL structure, generating client stubs with wsimport or Spring-WS, creating simple SOAP endpoints with @Endpoint and @PayloadRoot, and marshalling/unmarshalling XML with JAXB. They integrate persistence through Spring Data JPA or Spring JDBC Template, configure pooled or embedded DataSource, annotate service methods with @Transactional, perform CRUD operations, map entities to DTOs (manual, ModelMapper, MapStruct), and run SQL/Flyway scripts for schema evolution. They externalize configuration in application.properties or YAML, activate dev/test/prod profiles, employ SLF4J with Logback (MDC aware), and configure basic Spring Security for API authentication (HTTP Basic, API keys, simple JWT validation). They build with Maven or Gradle, manage dependency scopes, package as JAR or WAR, and run embedded servers via Spring Boot. They write unit tests in JUnit 5, mock collaborators with Mockito, test REST endpoints through MockMvc and TestRestTemplate, use WireMock for stubbing external service calls, and enforce quality with SpotBugs/PMD/Checkstyle and JaCoCo. They troubleshoot using application logs, Spring debug traces, Postman/curl/browser DevTools, and recognise common production risks including connection pool exhaustion, timeout cascades, and serialization errors. They participate in story estimation, code reviews, API documentation (SpringDoc/OpenAPI/Swagger), and concise Markdown/JavaDoc documentation to contribute smoothly within a team.
